title: Commutative property of multiplication
description: Practice changing the order of factors in a multiplication problem and see how it affects the product.

hints:
$\blueD3\times\purpleC5$ is equal to $\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC5$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC5$ has the same total as $\blueD5$ groups of $\purpleC3$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$3 \times 5 = \blueD5\times 3$

$\blueD2\times\purpleC7$ is equal to $\blueD2$ groups of $\purpleC7$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$ groups of $\purpleC7$ has the same total as $\blueD7$ groups of $\purpleC2$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$2 \times 7 = \blueD7\times 2$

$\blueD4\times\purpleC3$ is equal to $\blueD4$ groups of $\purpleC3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD4$ groups of $\purpleC3$ has the same total as $\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC4$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$4 \times 3 = \blueD3\times 4$

$\blueD6\times\purpleC4$ is equal to $\blueD6$ groups of $\purpleC4$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD6$ groups of $\purpleC4$ has the same total as $\blueD4$ groups of $\purpleC6$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$6 \times 4 = 4\times \purpleC6$

$\blueD7\times\purpleC3$ is equal to $\blueD7$ groups of $\purpleC3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD7$ groups of $\purpleC3$ has the same total as $\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC7$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$7\times 3 = 3\times \purpleC7$

$\blueD8\times\purpleC2$ is equal to $\blueD8$ groups of $\purpleC2$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD8$ groups of $\purpleC2$ has the same total as $\blueD2$ groups of $\purpleC8$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$8\times 2 = 2\times \purpleC8$

$\blueD8\times\purpleC4$ is equal to $\blueD8$ groups of $\purpleC4$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD8$ groups of $\purpleC4$ has the same total as $\blueD4$ groups of $\purpleC8$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$\blueD4\times 8 = 8\times4$

$\blueD4\times\purpleC5$ is equal to $\blueD4$ groups of $\purpleC5$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD4$ groups of $\purpleC5$ has the same total as $\blueD5$ groups of $\purpleC4$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$\blueD5\times 4 = 4\times5$

$\blueD6\times\purpleC3$ is equal to $\blueD6$ groups of $\purpleC3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD6$ groups of $\purpleC3$ has the same total as $\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC6$. 

 

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$3\times \purpleC6 = 6\times3$

$\blueD7\times\purpleC4$ is equal to $\blueD7$ groups of $\purpleC4$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD7$ groups of $\purpleC4$ has the same total as $\blueD4$ groups of $\purpleC7$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$4\times \purpleC7 = 7\times4$

$\blueD2\times\purpleC4$ is equal to $\blueD2$ groups of $\purpleC4$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$ groups of $\purpleC4$ has the same total as $\purpleC4$ groups of $\blueD2$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$2\times4 = \purpleC4\times2$

$\blueD6\times\purpleC8$ is equal to $\blueD6$ groups of $\purpleC8$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD6$ groups of $\purpleC8$ has the same total as $\purpleC8$ groups of $\blueD6$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$6\times8 = \purpleC8\times6$

$\blueD6\times\purpleC2$ is equal to $\blueD6$ groups of $\purpleC2$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD6$ groups of $\purpleC2$ has the same total as $\purpleC2$ groups of $\blueD6$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$6\times2 = \purpleC2\times6$

$\blueD3\times\purpleC8$ is equal to $\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC8$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC8$ has the same total as $\purpleC8$ groups of $\blueD3$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$3\times8 = \purpleC8\times3$

$\blueD9\times\purpleC4$ is equal to $\blueD9$ groups of $\purpleC4$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD9$ groups of $\purpleC4$ has the same total as $\purpleC4$ groups of $\blueD9$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$9\times4 = 4\times\blueD9$

$\blueD5\times\purpleC2$ is equal to $\blueD5$ groups of $\purpleC2$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD5$ groups of $\purpleC2$ has the same total as $\purpleC2$ groups of $\blueD5$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$5\times2 = 2\times\blueD5$

$\blueD3\times\purpleC2$ is equal to $\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC2$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC2$ has the same total as $\purpleC2$ groups of $\blueD3$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$3\times2 = 2\times\blueD3$

$\blueD3\times\purpleC9$ is equal to $\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC9$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC9$ has the same total as $\purpleC9$ groups of $\blueD3$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$\purpleC9\times3 = 3\times9$

$\blueD3\times\purpleC{10}$ is equal to $\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC{10}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD3$ groups of $\purpleC{10}$ has the same total as $\purpleC{10}$ groups of $\blueD{3}$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$10\times\blueD3 = 3\times10$

$\blueD2\times\purpleC{10}$ is equal to $\blueD2$ groups of $\purpleC{10}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD2$ groups of $\purpleC{10}$ has the same total as $\purpleC{10}$ groups of $\blueD{2}$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$\purpleC{10}\times2 = 2\times10$

$\blueD5\times\purpleC{1}$ is equal to $\blueD5$ groups of $\purpleC{1}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD5$ groups of $\purpleC{1}$ has the same total as $\purpleC{1}$ group of $\blueD{5}$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$1\times\blueD5 = 5\times1$
